user.title: AirPrint
user.description: Enter the address of the machine running the AirPrint add-on.

driver.title: {name} needs a driver
driver.description:
No driver for **{name}** is available, so it cannot print yet.

Most printers are matched automatically; yours needs one from its manufacturer. Paste a link to the driver — a `.deb`, a `.ppd`, or the vendor's `.tar.gz`.

You can leave this empty and add it later in the add-on's Drivers option.

data_description.driver: A direct link to the driver file. Host it yourself if you can — vendor links break when they publish an update.

data_description.name: Printer name. Default: {default}
data_description.device: Which printer on your network to print to.

driver.title: {name} needs a driver
driver.description:
No driver for **{name}** is available, so it cannot print yet.

Most printers are matched automatically; yours needs one from its manufacturer. Paste a link to the driver — a `.deb`, a `.ppd`, or the vendor's `.tar.gz`.

You can leave this empty and add it later in the add-on's Drivers option.

data_description.driver: A direct link to the driver file. Host it yourself if you can — vendor links break when they publish an update.

abort.already_configured: That printer is already set up.

error.driver_failed: Could not add the driver to the add-on.

no_driver.title: {name} needs a driver
no_driver.description:
Home Assistant has no driver for **{name}**, so it cannot print.

Most printers are matched automatically. Yours needs a driver from its manufacturer: add it to the **Drivers** option of the AirPrint add-on, then restart the add-on.
